review_notes: The functions provided are generally accurate and well-named, mapping appropriately to Kubernetes security controls related to boundary protection. However, the function 'k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted' could be more descriptive to indicate what specific restriction is being applied, such as limiting external exposure. The function names follow the established pattern and are consistent with the naming conventions. The functions effectively address the control's requirement to prevent unauthorized connections by using network policies and securing ingress traffic.
title: Boundary Protection - Boundary protection | classified national security system connections prohibit t
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7(26) focuses on boundary protection, specifically prohibiting unauthorized connections to classified systems. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that network policies are in place to deny all ingress and egress traffic by default, thus preventing unauthorized access. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services and ensuring that Ingress resources have TLS enabled can help secure the boundary by limiting exposure and encrypting data in transit.
